name: Seed1.5-Thinking
note: RL-trained reasoning MoE matching o3-mini-high on AIME24; served via Volcano Engine API only.

results.notes: ARC-AGI score of 39.9% from the official Seed-Thinking-v1.5 README comparison table (Seed1.5-Thinking 39.9% vs DeepSeek R1 18.3%, OpenAI o3-mini 25.8%, Grok 3 Beta 31.9%, Gemini 2.5 Pro 27.6%). The table labels it simply 'ARC-AGI' without specifying v1 or v2, and the arXiv paper (2504.13914) body text does not mention ARC-AGI at all. Mapped to arc-agi-1 rather than arc-agi-2 because the score magnitude is only consistent with ARC-AGI-1: as of April 2025 all four comparison models (DeepSeek R1, o3-mini, Grok 3, Gemini 2.5 Pro) scored under ~10% on ARC-AGI-2's semi-private eval, whereas 18-32% scores for this same group are squarely in the known ARC-AGI-1 range for that period. Flagging this inference for reviewer scrutiny given the corpus's known variant-conflation risk.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real: arXiv 2504.13914 (initial submission April 10, 2025, matching releaseDate), GitHub repo ByteDance-Seed/Seed-Thinking-v1.5, and official ByteDance Seed blog all corroborate. Params 200B total/20B active MoE confirmed in the paper abstract. AIME24 86.7, GPQA 77.3, Codeforces (pass@8) 55.0, and LiveCodeBench v5 64.9 all confirmed reading the paper/GitHub page directly. VentureBeat coverage independently confirms the 'matching o3-mini-high on AIME24' comparison. CORRECTION: dropped the unverified claim that this model is 'aka doubao-1.5-thinking-pro' — fetched ByteDance's own Seed1.5-Thinking blog post directly and it does not state this equivalence (only that Doubao 1.5 Pro's training data was reused); softened the note field accordingly. Also downgraded 'GPQA Diamond' notes label to plain 'GPQA' since the source only says 'GPQA' without specifying the Diamond variant. Pricing figures ($0.556/$2.222) could not be independently pinned to a specific fetched Volcano Engine pricing page for this exact model ID and are carried over with lower confidence; ctxK of 128K likewise not directly confirmed for this text model (source used is a general Volcano Engine docs page, not model-specific).

2026-07-16: Researched the 5 missing core-benchmark gaps (mmlu, hle, math, humaneval, mmmu). Checked (1) official sources -- arXiv 2504.13914 abstract, full HTML body/tables (both v1 and v2, Table 2's complete results grid), the ByteDance Seed blog technical-details post, and the GitHub README comparison table; (2) independent leaderboards -- vals.ai model list (no ByteDance Seed entry at all as of this check), Epoch AI's benchmarking-hub search (zero results for "seed"), and llm-stats.com (only later Seed 2.0/2.1 models are tracked, not Seed1.5-Thinking); (3) sibling-model cross-reference within this corpus -- doubao-1-5-pro.json, doubao-1st-gen.json, doubao-seed-1-6.json, seed-2-1-pro.json, and seed-oss-36b.json were all inspected for any comparison-table row citing Seed1.5-Thinking on these five benchmarks; none exists (each sibling's mmlu/hle/math figures are that sibling's own self-reported number, not a comparison-table citation of Seed1.5-Thinking). Conclusion for each: mmlu -- ByteDance's official Table 2 reports only MMLU-Pro (87.0, already in corpus), never base MMLU; treated as a deliberate substitution, not a gap. hle -- Humanity's Last Exam is entirely absent from the paper/README/blog; HLE didn't become a standard frontier-eval fixture in ByteDance's Seed line until later releases (Seed-OSS-36B, Seed2.1-Pro both added it months afterward), consistent with an April-2025 model simply predating that convention. math -- the paper's math section reports AIME 2024/2025 and the internal 'Beyond AIME' set only; standard MATH/MATH-500 is not evaluated, likely considered saturated/superseded by AIME for a frontier reasoning model. humaneval -- superseded by LiveCodeBench v5 and Codeforces in this model's coding suite; HumanEval is not mentioned anywhere in the paper. mmmu -- this model's corpus record has capabilities.vision = false and modalities = [\"text\"] (Seed1.5-Thinking is a text-only reasoning model, distinct from the separately released Seed1.5-VL vision-language model); MMMU is a vision benchmark and is correctly inapplicable, not merely unpublished. All five are omitted_no_evidence; no scores added or altered.
